page: OVHcloud h100-1520 is a KVM-based virtual server belonging to the Cloud GPU family. It features an x86_64 architecture with 120 dedicated vCPUs operating at a base clock speed of 3.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 1520.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 12.67 GB of RAM per core.

This server configuration includes four NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs, delivering a total of 320 GB of high-bandwidth VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 4040 GB NVMe SSD. The network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Given the high density of enterprise-grade hardware, including multiple H100 accelerators and a substantial memory footprint, this instance represents a high-resource tier designed for massive parallel processing. The server is engineered for demanding workloads such as large language model training, deep learning, complex data science jobs, and high-performance computing tasks that require significant GPU memory and fast local storage.
description: OVHcloud h100-1520 is a high-resource virtual server in the Cloud GPU family, running on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 120 dedicated vCPUs clocked at 3.0 GHz, 1520.0 GB of system memory, and a 4040 GB NVMe SSD for fast local storage. The primary hardware feature is its array of four NVIDIA Hopper H100 GPUs, providing a combined 320 GB of VRAM. With a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps and one complimentary public IPv4 address, this server is designed for resource-intensive workloads. The massive GPU memory and high-performance compute capabilities make it suitable for large-scale machine learning, deep learning training, and complex data science simulations.
